title: Who has seen the wind?
tags: jan130 tree grasses clouds sky suttonpark nearbannersgate february2020 landscape topazstudio digitalpainting windymorning walkswithmarnie poem wind vento vent viento ветар
description:
Out on a windy but wonderfully sunny Saturday morning, and I don't think the wind has dropped its power since the day I took this photo two weeks ago.  We seem to be in its path wherever we are, it's merciless !  Looks set to be the same for a few days yet but without any sun.  Come on Spring !!!

'Who has seen the wind?
Neither I nor you:
But when the leaves hang trembling,
The wind is passing through.

Who has seen the wind?
Neither you nor I:
But when the trees bow down their heads,
The wind is passing by.'

- Christina Rossetti 

Thank you as always for your kind comments - or just for looking.  Every view is appreciated, as I'm sure you know ! 

~ Edited in Topaz Studio ~

title: A new day begins
tags: jan130 walkswithmarnie sunshine joyful unexpectedsunshine music topazstudio suttonpark englanduk femaledog japanesespitz
description:
Shooting straight into the sun, I hope you like my picture of Marnie, gazing up the hill, as a new day and hopefully, a better life for so many, begins. It rained for most of the day after this, but this is November in England !

I chose this music to go with my image ... a famous song, and a fantastic one, with a lot of meaning in the lyrics. 

<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D5Y11hwjMNs" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Nina Simone - Feeling Good</a>

Edited with Topaz Studio and PicMonkey but only because I like doing it when I have time.

We enjoy what happiness we find in our lives to its full extent.  This was certainly a joy to see and to photograph.

Thanks so much for looking.  Every view and comment is deeply appreciated.

title: Dusk over the Lake
tags: jan130 lake trees water dusk sunset autumn2022 suttonpark suttoncoldfield englanduk blackrootpool waterbirds coth5
description:
A happy day, as I and my sons and grandchildren all got together near this beautiful lake for lunch and then had a long walk, as much as the children could manage !  This is Blackroot Pool, in Sutton Park, a National Nature Reserve in Sutton Coldfield, England.  It's only a 15 minute drive from home, depending on the traffic on the way there.  I shall go back soon when I'm not quite so busy at home !

Sutton Park is huge and it's easy to get lost there, as described in my last upload -- but there's a circular walk all around this lake which is a blessing !   Thankfully we only got lost in its beauty.

~ Filter applied in Topaz Studio and finishing touches in PicMonkey Pro ~

Thank you as always for your views, faves an comments.  As always, so very appreciated my friends here on Flickr.

title: Sutton Park in Autumn
tags: jan130 suttonpark suttoncoldfield england trees coth5 sunrays5
description:
Sutton Park is a 2,400 acre National Nature Reserve located 6 miles north of the Birmingham city centre. It’s one of the largest urban parks in Europe and is a Site of Special Scientific Interest.
The park has open heathland, woodlands, seven lakes, wetlands, and marshes - each with its own rich variety of plants and wildlife, some rarely seen in the region. Cattle and wild ponies graze on the land.
Sutton Park has been designated as a National Nature Reserve, a Scheduled Ancient Monument and a Site of Special Scientific Interest.

I'm looking forward to the time I can go back there with Marnie, it was one her first real 'outings' with me when she first came to live with us.

title: The Kindness of Strangers
tags: jan130 suttonpark walkwithmarnie trees sunlight dog japanesespitz marnie trust kindness love kindnessofstrangers coth5
description:
We got lost !  We went to Sutton Park to look for the wild Exmoor ponies there.  Not a familiar part of the park, which is enormous.  We wandered around, looking (but not finding) ponies and I suddenly realised I didn't have a clue where the car was.  Marnie put her trust in me - I failed her !   We eventually found the right spot after asking three people who all admitted they often get lost in Sutton Park.  I am very grateful for the strangers who helped us out yesterday !

I feel like I'm failing her again this evening.  It's the annual celebration of  &quot;Guy Fawkes night&quot;.  Fireworks have been going off for many evenings now and she's once again terrified of the ever louder explosions.  

Please.... if you are celebrating anything involving fireworks, do remember the animals who can't understand it, not just domestic animals, but wildlife.  

Many die of shock during this time.  Fireworks are not fun for them.  They're a horrific experience.

Thank you for looking.  Have a great weekend but please, be kind. 

Many, many thanks to all of you who helped my picture into Explore on 6th November 2022 !

title: The Cormorant on the Lake
tags: jan130 lake trees water bird sunrise landscape cormorant texture january2020 topazstudio poem wyndleypool suttonpark suttoncoldfield englanduk ngc npc
description:
dries its wings in the typical cormorant pose.  I don't know if this is one of the birds in my last upload, but I very much like this poem about cormorants.  I have known it since I was a child - perhaps you do too !

The common cormorant (or shag)
Lays eggs inside a paper bag,
You follow the idea, no doubt?
It's to keep the lightning out.

But what these unobservant birds
Have never thought of, is that herds
Of wandering bears might come with buns
And steal the bags to hold the crumbs. 

-- Christopher Isherwood

~ Processed in Topaz Studio with a gorgeous texture from Cris Buscaglia Lenz - thanks so much Cris ! ~

title: Beeches in the fog
tags: jan130 winter2021 january2021 beechtrees fog mist atmospheric peace walkswithmarnie suttoncoldfield suttonpark nationalnaturereserve topaz ngc npc
description:
Or Mad about mist, I headed out to Sutton Park this morning with Marnie.  At first, we could barely see 10 yards around us, but when we reached here, near the Donkey Sanctuary, the fog started to lift a little, enough to get a fairly 'atmospheric' photo.  It was very quiet, very peaceful.  We enjoyed it.  I may not be able to visit the mountains, but there are always the beautiful woods so near home.  I am privileged to be only three miles away.  Others have nowhere to roam in safety.  I am also so blessed to have a loving canine member of my family who takes me out for walks with her.

~ Edited in Picasa, Topaz Studio and the new PicMonkey ~

As always, thank you very much for your views and comments - I appreciate every one of you so much.

title: Where Romans once crossed
tags: streetly walsall railway train westmidlands england greatbritain uk 2022 class66 dieselelectric locomotive emd generalmotors gm 66775 4h61 gbrf gbrailfreight romanroad icknieldstreet covid19pandemic 202203155295101 suttonparkline
description:
Class 66 locomotive No. 66775 approaches bridge No. 45 at Streetly in the West Midlands with 4H61, the 09:52 Small Heath to Hindlow working on Tuesday 15th March 2022.

The viewpoint is from a foot crossing on the alignment of Icknield Street, the Roman road that ran from Templeborough in South Yorkshire to a junction with the Fosse Way at Bourton on the Water in Gloucestershire.  The section here through Sutton Park is well preserved and can quite easily be traced today.  This surviving fragment of the ancient road is - as you might expect - perfectly straight.

title: The Sweet, sweet chestnut tree ( Explore )
tags: jan130 digitalpainting suttonpark trees sweetchestnut spanishchestnut coth5
description:
Another one from our visit to Sutton Park, this time with an artistic touch.

Please don't confuse sweet chestnuts with horse chestnuts, which are poisonous.  The sweet chestnut burr is brown when ripe and has numerous long bristly spines (as seen here). It contains two to three nuts at a time, which are fairly small, flattened and triangular, delicious roasted !  Horse chestnut burrs are thick and green, with small, short, more widely spaced spikes, and generally contain only one large rounded nut.

~ Edited in Topaz Impression - Cezanne ~

Thank you for every view and comment my Flickr friends.  Enjoy autumn / fall while it's here 😃 !

title: Magic for Marnie ( Explored )
tags: jan130 landscape textured animal mammal rainbow suttonpark picmonkey path trees coth coth5 sunrays5
description:
Today is Marnie's 10th birthday, so I took her to one of the most loved expeditions we used to make when she was first with us, five years ago.  This is her favourite place in Sutton Park, and she was so very happy to be there !  She remembered everything and had a lovely time.  Everything was perfect ! 

Here she is, looking on as a rainbow appeared.  She didn't wait for me, she headed straight towards it 😃

I didn't wait either, only stopped to take this quick shot of it all.

Thank you my Flickr friends for every view, comment and fave.  May your days be filled with wonder, love and magic too.

title: Black Root
tags: blackroot overbridge railway train suttonparkline suttoncoldfield england greatbritain uk 2021 birmingham westmidlands class66 dieselelectric locomotive emd gm generalmotors 66710 gbrailfreight gbrf covid19pandemic 2021080705372101
description:
Class 66 locomotive No. 66710 approaches the overbridge at Black Root on the northern edge of Sutton Park with 4L17, the 15:04 Birch Coppice to Tilbury working on Saturday 7th August 2021.

When I arrived here some thirty minutes earlier I was greeted by the sight of a murder of crows fluttering around the bridge parapet.  I say crows, but I am not 100% sure that that's what they were.  On the other hand I am reasonably certain that the black birds I saw were members of the <i>Corvus</i> genus.  Maybe one day I will learn to distinguish between crows, ravens and rooks.

title: A Song for Freedom
tags: jan130 landscape trees suttonpark topazstudio warmweather walkswithmarnie latefeb2021 earlyspring path heathland suttoncoldfield englanduk music sssi scheduledancientmonument ngc npc coth5
description:
I'm sure you can imagine the feeling walking through this lovely place with Marnie a week ago.  The sun was shining, it was warm and so inviting.  The weather's not as nice as this now - dropped down about 10 degrees C from then.  Ah well, Spring is coming !  Everyone I meet is a bit fed up with all this cold and damp though, come on Spring !

Here's some music I've grown quite passionate about through my journeys through YouTube ... I hope you like it too, oh, and my painted park, which is a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) and Scheduled Ancient Monument.

<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7k07j7LcLqw" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Gary Moore - The Messiah Will Come Again (live)</a>

~ Edited in Topaz Studio with finishing touches in PicMonkey ~

Keep safe... we're on the road to freedom now.  We have to look after ourselves in other ways too - thinking of all those who suffer from other illnesses which have been neglected because of the virus. 

Thanks for looking and listening my friends.  I am sorry that sometimes I'm so busy I can't keep up with your kindness - but thank you.  So much.

title: September Song
tags: jan130 leaves autumn fall september2020 autunno lautomne otoño outono herfst herbst topazstudio ngc npc
description:
Yes, it's autumn now.  Spring and summer are gone and the leaves are turning.  Seen in some woods in Sutton Park on a morning walk with Marnie.

<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k8p3L18yTg8" rel="noreferrer nofollow">September Song - by Frank Sinatra</a>

Please right click and open in a new tab.  This was probably recorded before I was born, but still my favourite version since I first heard it as a little girl.  What a beautiful voice he had then ...

~ Edited in Topaz Studio with the finishing touches applied in PicMonkey ~ 

Thank you for looking everyone, I hope you have a lovely Friday and weekend !

Many many thanks to everyone who helped this into Explore.... I am overwhelmed !

title: A Place for Us
tags: jan130 marnieshill topazstudio texturedimage frame suttonpark suttoncoldfield englanduk light
description:
We've come here 3 mornings in a row this week, as Marnie (my lovely dog) hasn't been very well and we both need peace and quiet and open air - she knows her hill well.  We met only a few people with their dogs, all very nice.  This is the second one from my new phone (which I still don't understand - limited time to learn new technology), but I'll keep trying !

I used texture 396 Sun by <a href="https://www.flickr.com/photos/lenabem-anna">Lenabem-Anna J.</a> Thank you so much Anna.

Thank you in advance for your views and comments - have a happy week ahead !

title: Yesterday
tags: jan130 autumn2025 trees autumnleaves suttonpark november2025 forest nationalnaturereserve sssi englanduk sunrays5 coth5 bcrazygeniusespublicb
description:
for the first time since Marnie died, I went to the woods in Sutton Park, my local big park, alone.  I missed her company, but I have to start going out again, so steeled myself.  I'm glad I went, and I hope you like the autumn colours as much as I did, who knows how long they will last?  Autumn is over so soon!

My picture edited slightly in Topaz Studio. 

Thank you for looking, my Flickr friends.  

Every view and comment is so appreciated !

title: Back to the Woods (explore - thank you !)
tags: jan130 autumn2025 suttonpark nationalnaturereserve autumntrees sssi forest woods suttoncoldfield englanduk coth5 sunrays5
description:
Here I am, back in the woods again, enjoying the leaf carpet during a sunny early afternoon.  There are still colourful leaves on the trees, and yesterday's rain didn't make too much mud !

My picture is textured with two of my own textures. 

I do hope you like it !

Thank you as always for every view, my Flickr friends.

Happy December 😃 everyone !

title: Blackroot Pool, Sutton Park
tags: suttoncoldfield royalsuttoncoldfield theroyaltownofsuttoncoldfield birmingham westmidlands england unitedkingdom greatbritain suttonpark tree trees nationalnaturereserve scheduledancientmonument blackrootpool
description:
Another trip to Sutton Park. This time starting from the Four Oaks Gate in Four Oaks, Sutton Coldfield.


<a href="https://sp.scnhs.org.uk/lakes.html" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Sutton Park lakes</a>


Blackroot Pool

Blackroot Pool was built in the 18th century with the primary aim of powering a watermill. The lease which allowed the pool to be constructed was granted by the Sutton Corporation in 1757 for 2 shillings (£0.10), well below what appears to have been the 'market rate'. However, the lessees were the Corporation's Warden and his nephew. The mill was initially used for leather dressing, but later became a water-powered saw mill. The modern saw mill is used to make gates, fences and other timber products from timber produced within Sutton Park. The pool is approximately 12 acres (5 hectares) in size. Angling is permitted in the pool; the main species of fish found here are Bream, Roach, Carp and Pike.

Another bridge over the Sutton Park Line.


<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Park,_West_Midlands#Railway_station" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Sutton Park railway station</a>

A railway line, the Sutton Park Line, was built through the Park in 1879 and the Park had its own station. The advent of the railway, and the new town entrance, greatly increased the number of visitors to the Park. The Park's own dedicated station was closed in 1964, and the line now only serves goods trains.

Construction of the line resulted in the filling in of an area of wetland known as &quot;Webb's Stows&quot;, causing the loss of several species of rare plants from the park.

title: Bracebridge Pool, Sutton Park
tags: suttoncoldfield royalsuttoncoldfield theroyaltownofsuttoncoldfield birmingham westmidlands england unitedkingdom greatbritain suttonpark tree trees nationalnaturereserve scheduledancientmonument bracebridgepool sirralphbracebridge birminghamuk
description:
Another trip to Sutton Park. This time starting from the Four Oaks Gate in Four Oaks, Sutton Coldfield.

<a href="https://sp.scnhs.org.uk/lakes.html" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Sutton Park lakes</a>

Bracebridge Pool

Bracebridge Pool is one of the largest in the park. The views as you approach this pool are also amongst the finest in Sutton Park. The pool takes its name from Sir Ralph Bracebridge. Bracebridge obtained a life lease on the Chase of Sutton Coldfield in 1419 and had the pool constructed to provide a good supply of bream for his family and friends. The Boat House Restaurant can be found at the edge of Bracebridge Pool. The pool is approximately 16 acres (6.5 hectares) in size. Angling is permitted in the pool; the main species of fish found here are Roach, Tench and Pike.

title: Deer Park Subvisison in Sutton Park - path
tags: suttoncoldfield royalsuttoncoldfield theroyaltownofsuttoncoldfield birmingham westmidlands england unitedkingdom greatbritain suttonpark tree trees nationalnaturereserve scheduledancientmonument deerparksubdivision path
description:
A walk around <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Park,_West_Midlands" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Sutton Park</a> on a sunny and warm early June 2021 morning. From the Town Gate to the Boldmere Gate, via the Keepers Pool and Keepers Well.

Too large to do the whole park in one go. Plus wanted to walk back into Sutton Coldfield Town Centre.


I went a little bit further than Keepers Pool and Well.

Was a marker for the Deer Park Subdivision.

After this I changed route towards the Boldmere Gate.


It was the remains of a boundary consisting of a bank and ditch. It was constructed in the 12th Century to create an enclosure, to the right of the boundary, into which deer were rounded up for hunting. The bank would have had a hedge or fence on top of it. Over time the bank has subsided and the ditch has filled in.

title: Blackroot Pool, Sutton Park - Bird kite bench boat
tags: suttoncoldfield royalsuttoncoldfield theroyaltownofsuttoncoldfield birmingham westmidlands england unitedkingdom greatbritain suttonpark tree trees nationalnaturereserve scheduledancientmonument blackrootpool birdkitebenchboat
description:
Another trip to Sutton Park. This time starting from the Four Oaks Gate in Four Oaks, Sutton Coldfield.


<a href="https://sp.scnhs.org.uk/lakes.html" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Sutton Park lakes</a>


Blackroot Pool

Blackroot Pool was built in the 18th century with the primary aim of powering a watermill. The lease which allowed the pool to be constructed was granted by the Sutton Corporation in 1757 for 2 shillings (£0.10), well below what appears to have been the 'market rate'. However, the lessees were the Corporation's Warden and his nephew. The mill was initially used for leather dressing, but later became a water-powered saw mill. The modern saw mill is used to make gates, fences and other timber products from timber produced within Sutton Park. The pool is approximately 12 acres (5 hectares) in size. Angling is permitted in the pool; the main species of fish found here are Bream, Roach, Carp and Pike.


Bird kite bench boat

title: Sutton Park Line near the Blackroot Pool
tags: suttoncoldfield royalsuttoncoldfield theroyaltownofsuttoncoldfield birmingham westmidlands england unitedkingdom greatbritain suttonpark tree trees nationalnaturereserve scheduledancientmonument suttonparkline bridge rail rails track tracks
description:
Another trip to Sutton Park. This time starting from the Four Oaks Gate in Four Oaks, Sutton Coldfield.


Sutton Park Line near the Blackroot Pool

Another bridge over the Sutton Park Line.


<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Park,_West_Midlands#Railway_station" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Sutton Park railway station</a>

A railway line, the Sutton Park Line, was built through the Park in 1879 and the Park had its own station. The advent of the railway, and the new town entrance, greatly increased the number of visitors to the Park. The Park's own dedicated station was closed in 1964, and the line now only serves goods trains.

Construction of the line resulted in the filling in of an area of wetland known as &quot;Webb's Stows&quot;, causing the loss of several species of rare plants from the park.

Construction of the line resulted in the filling in of an area of wetland known as &quot;Webb's Stows&quot;, causing the loss of several species of rare plants from the park.


Walking Through Time sign 

5.1 Railway Line and Midland Lodge

title: Bracebridge Pool, Sutton Park
tags: suttoncoldfield royalsuttoncoldfield theroyaltownofsuttoncoldfield birmingham westmidlands england unitedkingdom greatbritain suttonpark tree trees nationalnaturereserve scheduledancientmonument bracebridgepool sirralphbracebridge
description:
Another trip to Sutton Park. This time starting from the Four Oaks Gate in Four Oaks, Sutton Coldfield.

<a href="https://sp.scnhs.org.uk/lakes.html" rel="noreferrer nofollow">Sutton Park lakes</a>

Bracebridge Pool

Bracebridge Pool is one of the largest in the park. The views as you approach this pool are also amongst the finest in Sutton Park. The pool takes its name from Sir Ralph Bracebridge. Bracebridge obtained a life lease on the Chase of Sutton Coldfield in 1419 and had the pool constructed to provide a good supply of bream for his family and friends. The Boat House Restaurant can be found at the edge of Bracebridge Pool. The pool is approximately 16 acres (6.5 hectares) in size. Angling is permitted in the pool; the main species of fish found here are Roach, Tench and Pike.

Keepers Pool  - it dates to the 15th Century.

There was a rare 1887 lido, for open-air all-weather swimming, at Keepers Pool. But the lido closed in 2003 after arson, and was burned down entirely in 2004. The Lido area has now been purposely reverted to woodland and wetland.

title: Jamboree Stone
tags: suttonpark jamboreestone scouts plaque geo:lat=5257137889 geo:lon=185708078 geotagged suttoncoldfield uk
description:
The Jamboree Stone commemorates the 1957 World Jamboree held in the Park. A second plaque was added to commemorate the centenary of the Scout movement in 2007.

&quot;This Plaque commemorates 100 years since the founding of the Scout movement by Lord Baden-Powell of Gilwell and 50 years since the World Scout Jubilee Jamboree was held in this park.

In 1907 just 20 Scouts attended the first camp on Brownsea Isalnd in Dorset. Today there are over 28 million scouts worldwide. A Fitting tribute to Baden-Powell's vision.&quot;
Sutton Park

title: Rowton's Well
tags: rowtonswell holywell suttonpark birmingham
description: We only went to Sutton Park because the car was being serviced nearby and we needed to kill some time.  We discovered that it is full of fascinating things, including Rowton's Well, which has a long history of being regarded as a magical place. You can see the water bubbling out of the ground and the general area was quite arresting at the back end of autumn.  I came back a couple of days later, on a stormy day, with a tripod.
